chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Cr. B.A. No.3250 of 2025 (Mst. Dur Bibi vs. The State) For hearing of bail application Date of hearing & order 26.03.2026 Mr. Hyder Farooq Jatoi, advocate for applicant Ms. Rubina Qadir, DPG O R D E R ----- Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro, J:- Applicant is seeking post arrest bail in a case bearing Crime No.05/2025, u/s 6/9-1F & 6/9-2 of CNS Amended Act, 2024, registered at Police Station Excise, Central, Karachi by means of this application. 2. Applicant was arrested on spy information on 19.09.2025 from Bus Stop Laloo Khait, near Dak Khana Shara-e-Pakistan, District Central, Karachi. Upon her personal search, 500 grams of heroin and 250 grams of ice were recovered. Then during interrogation, on her pointation, a further recovery of 4,240 grams of ice and 10 kilograms of heroin was effected from Flat No. C-8, 1st Floor, Rufi Lake Drive Apartment, Gulistan-e-Jauhar, Karachi. Hence, she was booked in the present case. 3. Learned counsel for the applicant has pleaded for bail on the ground that she is an old lady aged about 85 old years, and no independent persons have been cited as witnesses in the present case. 4. His arguments have been rebutted by learned DPG. There are prima facie reasonable grounds to believe involvement of the applicant in the case. From her a huge quantity of narcotics was recovered, which is supported by statements of witnesses. The offence is punishable for capital punishment. No ground for bail is made out. Accordingly, this bail application is dismissed. However, the learned trial court is directed to expedite the trial and conclude it in accordance with law as soon as possible 5.

Accordingly, this bail application is dismissed. However, the learned trial court is directed to expedite the trial and conclude it in accordance with law as soon as possible 5. Bail application stands disposed of in the above terms. The observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and would not prejudice case of either party at trial. JUDGE JUDGE Rafiq/PA.
